name: Wallace Davenport
abstract: Wallace Foster Davenport (30 June 1925 – 18 March 2004) was an American jazz trumpeter. Davenport has been one of the few traditional jazz musicians of the 1930s who later branched out into swing and bop styles, as well as backing gospel and R&B vocalists during an extensive career in eight different decades.

about_quotes.speaker: Player
about_quotes.evidence: I really enjoyed my time working with Wallace Davenport... I think I probably joined that band about ’96... all of these gentleman that I played with at that time... That band was Wallace Davenport

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: Wallace Davenport was in the band so he called me... Lionel Hampton needs a trumpet player for the night man... So we had a rehearsal that afternoon and made the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Allred
about_quotes.evidence: I met Wallace Davenport... and ended up doing two albums with him and would go down to New Orleans and sit in with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Allred interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: After I finished the big band job he asked me to play with this small group, and Wallace was the other trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: Wallace Davenport on trumpet... and yours truly, Arnette Cobb.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: Wallace Davenport and Wendell Brunious, and a few others, Wynton Marsallis and his brother are playing or taking up, the young blacks are coming back again with jazz more in the forefront.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
